steps.title: Spot the coordinate structure
steps.content: The participial phrase “studying connections between...” contains two noun phrases being coordinated: the physical location phrase and the post's content phrase. The blank must complete the first phrase's relative clause before “and” introduces the second.

steps.title: Apply the coordination rule
steps.content: Coordinating two items with “and” requires no comma before the conjunction—commas precede “and” only when joining independent clauses or in series of three or more items. Choice B correctly uses “created and the post's content” with no commas, while the other options violate this rule by adding commas either before “and” or after “content.” That matches choice B — B is correct.
